evals.prompt: Create a new Rancher UI extension called 'monitoring-extras' that adds a top-level product with a dashboard page showing cluster metrics summary.
evals.expected_output: A scaffolded extension with index.ts, product.ts, routing/extension-routing.ts, and a dashboard page component. Product uses inStore: 'management'. All files are TypeScript. Routing follows /<PRODUCT>/c/:cluster pattern.
expectations: index.ts calls importTypes(plugin) at the top of the register() function

index.ts uses require('./product') (NOT import product from './product') because the shell runtime looks up the .init property on the module object; a default-exported function has no .init and is silently ignored
index.ts calls plugin.addRoutes() with the default export of routing/extension-routing.ts
product.ts uses NAMED export function init(...) (not export default) and calls $extension.DSL(store, name) followed by product() for side-menu registration
routing/extension-routing.ts uses RouteRecordRaw from vue-router (vue-router v4 renamed RouteConfig -> RouteRecordRaw)
product.ts uses inStore: 'management' for top-level product
routing/extension-routing.ts defines routes with meta containing product and cluster params
Route names follow the pattern 'product-c-cluster-*'
Dashboard page component uses <script setup lang="ts"> (Composition API)
No raw HTML <input> or <select> elements in any component
All .vue files have <style lang="scss" scoped>

evals.prompt: Add a custom tab to the Deployment detail page in Rancher that shows pod restart statistics. The tab should appear in Cluster Explorer for all clusters.
evals.expected_output: A plugin.addTab() call in index.ts with TabLocation.RESOURCE_DETAIL_PAGE, a LocationConfig targeting apps.deployment in the explorer product, and a Vue component for the tab content.
expectations: Uses plugin.addTab() with TabLocation.RESOURCE_DETAIL_PAGE as the 'where' parameter
LocationConfig includes product: ['explorer'] and resource: ['apps.deployment']
Tab component uses <script setup lang="ts"> (Composition API, not Options API)
Tab component receives the resource as a prop
No custom save logic or API calls in the tab component (read-only display)
Does NOT use raw HTML <table> — uses Rancher components for layout

evals.prompt: Create an edit page for a custom CRD 'myapp.example.com' in my extension. The form should have fields for name, namespace, replicas (number), and image (text). Support create and edit modes.
evals.expected_output: An edit/myapp.example.com.vue file using CruResource wrapper and CreateEditView mixin. Form uses Rancher components (LabeledInput, NameNsDescription). Save goes through the standard CreateEditView.save() flow.
expectations: File is located at edit/myapp.example.com.vue (file name matches resource name)
Uses CruResource as the form wrapper component
Uses CreateEditView mixin (mixins: [CreateEditView])
Uses Options API with <script lang="ts"> (because of CreateEditView mixin)
CruResource has :resource, :mode, :errors props and @finish="save" event
Uses NameNsDescription component for name and namespace fields
Uses LabeledInput imported from @components/Form/LabeledInput/LabeledInput.vue for replicas and image fields
Does NOT implement a custom save button — relies on CruResource footer
Does NOT make direct API calls — save flow goes through CreateEditView.save() → SteveModel.save()
Does NOT use raw HTML <input>, <select>, or <textarea>
LabeledInput uses v-model binding to value.spec properties
Has :mode prop passed to all form components for view/edit state

evals.prompt: In my existing 'monitoring-extras' extension, add a panel above the Deployment list showing a warning banner about pod restart thresholds. Do not touch anything else in index.ts.
evals.expected_output: A single plugin.addPanel(PanelLocation.RESOURCE_LIST, ...) call appended to index.ts, plus a new panel Vue component. All existing addProduct/addRoutes calls remain unchanged.
expectations: Adds exactly one plugin.addPanel() call using PanelLocation.RESOURCE_LIST
PanelLocation is imported from @shell/core/types
LocationConfig contains resource: ['apps.deployment']
options.component uses dynamic import: () => import('./panels/...')
Existing plugin.addProduct / plugin.addRoutes calls in index.ts are not modified or reordered
Panel component uses <script setup lang="ts"> (Composition API)

evals.prompt: My extension crashes at load with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'DSL')`. What's wrong and how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Identifies that $extension.DSL(store, name) in product.ts is being called with an undefined store, most commonly because init(plugin, args) did not destructure store from args or the plugin was registered before the store was ready. Provides the corrected init signature.
expectations: Names product.ts as the file to inspect
Points at $extension.DSL(store, name) as the failing call site
Explains that 'store' is undefined and shows the correct init(plugin, { store }) destructuring
Does not suggest reinstalling dependencies or clearing caches as the primary fix

evals.prompt: Add a top-level product side-menu entry with a custom icon. The user tried `virtualType({ labelKey, name, route, icon: 'gear', weight: 100 })` and got TS2353.
evals.expected_output: Explains that `icon` is not a valid key on virtualType. Icons are set on the product itself via `product({ icon: 'gear', ... })`. For ordering, use `weightType()` / `weightGroup()` — not inline `weight` on virtualType (which happens to be accepted at runtime but is not part of the public TS type).
expectations: States that `icon` is NOT a valid key on virtualType (only on product)
Cites TS2353 as the compile-time signal that surfaces the mistake
Points at $extension.DSL(store, name).product({ icon: '...' }) as the correct place to set the icon
Recommends weightType()/weightGroup() over inline `weight` on virtualType for side-menu ordering
Notes the runtime accepts unknown keys via getTree obj spread, so the absence of a TS error is not proof of validity

evals.prompt: My route renders the shell 404 page when I navigate to it, even though the route is registered.
evals.expected_output: Guides the user to verify (1) the route `name` matches the `to.name` in product({...}) and the side-menu registration, and (2) inStore matches product scope. Adding `meta.pkg` is a last-resort in complex setups — most official examples omit `pkg` and render fine.
expectations: First checks route `name` vs product({ to.name }) match
Next checks side-menu registration references the same route name
Next checks inStore in product() matches the route meta (management vs cluster)
Treats adding meta.pkg as a last-resort workaround, not the first fix
Does not claim `pkg` is required

evals.prompt: Show me the canonical Rancher extension index.ts entry file.
evals.expected_output: Uses require('./product') and require('./package.json') for the shell runtime property-lookup pattern, and ESM `import extensionRouting from './routing/extension-routing'` for routes. Product init is a NAMED export (never default).
expectations: plugin.addProduct(require('./product')) — uses require, NOT ESM import (shell reads .init property; default import would bind to the function and load silently as no-op)
plugin.metadata = require('./package.json') — uses require for portability across bundlers
plugin.addRoutes(routes) — uses ESM `import routes from './routing/extension-routing'` (idiomatic; both styles work)
product.ts exports `function init` as a NAMED export (not default)

evals.prompt: My extension has a list view that fetches all Pods with `findAll`. On a cluster with 10,000+ pods the page hangs for seconds and memory balloons. How do I fix this while keeping backwards compatibility?
evals.expected_output: Identifies that from Rancher v2.12.0 Steve API supports Server-Side Pagination (SSP), gated by the `ui-sql-cache` feature flag. Advises probing per-type via `store.getters['cluster/paginationEnabled'](POD)` and branching: use `findPage` under SSP-on with filter/pagination opts, keep `findAll` as fallback under SSP-off. Notes both flag states must be manually tested.
expectations: Names Server-Side Pagination (SSP) and the `ui-sql-cache` feature flag
Recommends probing `store.getters['cluster/paginationEnabled'](POD)` (or `management/paginationEnabled` for management-scoped resources) before choosing the code path
Under SSP-on dispatches `cluster/findPage` with filters/pagination opts (from `shell/types/store/pagination.types.ts` FilterArgs)
Under SSP-off keeps `findAll` as the fallback branch
Explicitly calls out that BOTH flag states must be manually tested before shipping
Does NOT recommend using `findPage` unconditionally without the paginationEnabled probe
Notes SSP only applies to Steve API (/v1/*), not Norman (/v3/*) or native Kube (/apis/*)

evals.prompt: Add a form field to my extension's edit page where the user picks a Secret from the current namespace. The extension needs to work on clusters with tens of thousands of secrets.
evals.expected_output: Uses `ResourceLabeledSelect` (NOT `LabeledSelect`), configured via the `paginatedResourceSettings` prop to fetch a paginated slice of secrets filtered by the target namespace. Cites `shell/components/form/SecretSelector.vue` as the reference implementation and notes the component handles both SSP-on and SSP-off transparently.
expectations: Uses `ResourceLabeledSelect` (NOT `LabeledSelect`)
Passes `paginatedResourceSettings` prop configured with the resource type (SECRET) and namespace filter
Does NOT implement client-side filtering or client-side full-list fetching
References `shell/components/form/SecretSelector.vue` or `shell/chart/rancher-backup/S3.vue` as the pattern source
Notes ResourceLabeledSelect works transparently under both `ui-sql-cache` on and off
